profile_backing.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline linalg

lever.name: four-row register GEMM microkernel
lever.description: For full groups of four output rows, attempted to stream one B row once and update four C rows while preserving ascending-k accumulation for every output cell.

isomorphism_proof.per_cell_order: Each candidate output cell applied sum += a[i*k+l] * b[l*n+j] for l = 0..k-1, matching the existing i-k-j kernel and textbook i-j-k reference.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: No reassociation within any cell; adversarial bit test covered signed zero, infinities, and NaN payloads in candidate state.
isomorphism_proof.errors: No public error path changed; matmul_2d retains slice indexing behavior.

validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax matmul_2d -- --nocapture
validation.details: 3 tests passed in candidate state: basic matmul, bit-identical reference, adversarial bit-pattern test

rebench.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline linalg/matmul_2d_512x512x512_f64

next_primitive.target: actual packed B panels or cache-size KC blocking
next_primitive.reason: The register-only block reuses B but reloads A across column work and doubled runtime. The next deep GEMM attempt should pack a B panel and process a cache-sized KC block so both A and B reuse improve while preserving per-cell k order.
